g.text: bits 15–08 | stop_sig or exit_code | WSTOPSIG / WEXITSTATUS
bit 7 | core dump flag | WCOREDUMP (if WIFSIGNALED)
bits 6–00 | term_sig or 0x7f | WTERMSIG / WIFSTOPPED sentinel
Total: 32 bits — one int returned by waitpid(2)
Note: exec stop, attach stop, INT3 breakpoint
test | event != 0 → handle before syscall check
Note: WSTOPSIG still == SIGTRAP; event is in high byte
if sig == 0x85 | syscall stop (entry or exit) → handle_syscall_stop()
if sig == SIGTRAP | plain trap → resume, no action
if sig == SIGSTOP | new child initial stop → resume with 0
else | signal-delivery stop → re-inject: PTRACE_SYSCALL(pid, sig)
WARNING: never re-inject event stops; always pass 0
